name: River (Online ML)

performance.notes: Sub-millisecond per-sample prediction; constant memory regardless of stream size

agent_readiness.notes: Online machine learning library for Python. All models update incrementally with each new sample — constant memory regardless of stream size. Algorithms: Hoeffding trees, ADWIN, online regression/classification, anomaly detection, time series, clustering. Scikit-learn-compatible API. Pipeline support for feature extraction + model chaining. Best for real-time streaming data where batch training is impractical.

gotchas: Online algorithms converge slower than batch — need more samples for accuracy
Not a replacement for batch training when data fits in memory
Hyperparameter tuning differs from batch learning
best_when: Real-time streams where batch training is impractical (IoT sensors, logs)
Concept drift adaptation — models update automatically as data changes
Time series forecasting with online adaptation
avoid_when: When training data fits in memory (use scikit-learn for better accuracy)
Deep learning (River is classical ML only)
Complex feature engineering pipelines requiring distributed compute
summary: River is a Python library for online (streaming) machine learning. All models update incrementally with constant memory — perfect for real-time data streams where batch training is impractical. Algorithms include Hoeffding trees, ADWIN drift detection, online regression/classification, and time series forecasting. Scikit-learn-compatible API. Best for IoT, log analysis, and real-time recommendation systems.
